element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Dragon transformation abilities unlock progressively; core mechanic throughout adventure
Crossbow attacks, healing spells; forms core party early
Coliseum setting introduces combat scenarios; physical fighter
Bare-handed combat specialist; adds fighting variety
Exiled princess; represents higher-tier magic users; unique status as descendant of original Nina
Trick specialist; military background adds tactical dimension
Ninth playable character; minimal documentation suggests end-game acquisition
Text-based UI; indicates enemy encounter probability in areas; reduces random frustration
Every few steps in dangerous areas; core progression mechanic; turn-based combat resolution

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Early game, increases with party growth
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Each character has unique action for puzzle-solving, obstacle bypassing, area access; unlocks as party expands
Allows speed/defense/attack tradeoffs; tactical layer added to turn-based combat
Player populates custom village; currency-gated house additions; long-term progression reward
Six special inhabitants; up to two fuse with party members; grants new forms and battle abilities; elemental alignment strategic layer
Battery backup memory; placed throughout world; standard save mechanic

typical_relief_triggers: Towns where new party members join
typical_tension_triggers: Random enemy encounters every few steps
Party member limitations (only 4 in active party; restricted switching)
Character exiles and story obstacles (Nina's black wing curse)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive difficulty escalation with party expansion; alternates between exploration-driven tension (random encounters) and story-driven relief (character recruitment, village customization, shaman fusion moments)

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Town-building areas and Shaman recruitment locations function as optional reward spaces; Coliseum represents optional combat challenge

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with Ryu and Bow; Monster Meter provides encounter control; safe town exploration establishes mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Party expansion (Katt, Rand, Nina, Sten) increases combat complexity; Coliseum introduces specialized combat scenarios; Shaman system adds strategic layer; mandatory dangerous area traversal increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Bleu recruitment; full party (9 characters total) available though only 4 active; Shaman fusion at full capacity; town-building complete; higher encounter rates implied by story progression
notable_spikes: First mandatory dangerous area traversal after tutorial region
Nina recruitment context (exiled princess storyline implies tougher opposition)

notable_level_patterns.description: Towns serve as party recruitment hubs and dialogue/NPC interaction centers. Four-direction grid navigation. No random encounters. Represent relief and progression checkpoints.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Starting village (implied); towns where Bow, Katt, Rand, Nina, Sten join

notable_level_patterns.description: Open navigation (top-down 4-direction movement) with mandatory random enemy battles every few steps. Monster Meter indicates encounter probability. Progressive difficulty per dungeon.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Dangerous areas dictated by story progression

notable_level_patterns.description: Specialized combat-only environment where Katt joins. Removes exploration; pure turn-based battle sequencing. Introduces Formation system application.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Katt Coliseum (referenced in unused content as 'Katt Coliseum Corridor')

notable_level_patterns.description: Non-dangerous zone where player constructs houses and invites NPCs. Dialogue-driven character acquisition. Three carpenter NPCs with distinct building styles. Long-term progression reward.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Player-created village (central mechanic, location varies by player choices)

notable_level_patterns.description: Story locations where six special Shaman characters join town. Elemental alignment-based selection. Two-shaman capacity creates tactical build variety.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Locations implied by plot but not explicitly named in sources
